scope.summary:
GOAL: Author and execute the Interconnection Scenario Proof suite. This MT proves that the four core 'melt-together' interconnection edges declared in the WP-KERNEL-012 interconnection_contract actually work at runtime — not as assertions in unit isolation but as end-to-end scenarios that cross surface boundaries. These are the hardest proofs in the WP because they require multiple surfaces to be running simultaneously, sharing real state through the existing backend.

EXPECTED DIFF SHAPE: Creates the flat interconnection proof test files under src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/: (a) test_interconnect_manifest.json with one entry per scenario; (b) test_interconnect_ckc_to_note.rs (CKC media -> note embed scenarios); (c) test_interconnect_note_code_crossref.rs (note <-> code cross-reference scenarios); (d) test_interconnect_loom_backlink_search.rs (Loom backlink + search across surfaces); (e) test_interconnect_shared_undo_ledger.rs (shared undo + event-ledger scenarios). Each file tests a complete cross-surface workflow with real PG state, not mocked connections.

INTERCONNECTION EDGE 1 — CKC media -> note embed (test_interconnect_ckc_to_note.rs)

Scenario IC-01 — Drag CKC image into note: Simulate the drag-and-drop flow where a CKC media asset (stored as a real PG asset row) is dragged from the CKC/Studio surface into an open rich-text note. Expected result: the note's block document gains an hsLink node of kind 'HS_images' pointing to the asset's PG ID; the note is saved via PUT /workspaces/{id}/knowledge/documents/{doc_id}; loading the note back returns the hsLink node with the correct asset_id. Implementation: (1) create a test asset via POST /workspaces/{id}/assets (or direct DB insert via the storage layer); (2) call the native rich-editor API for InsertEmbed { kind: HsLinkKind::Image, ref_value: asset_id.to_string() }; (3) call save on the native editor, which calls PUT /workspaces/{id}/knowledge/documents/{doc_id}; (4) load the doc back, assert the hsLink node with kind='HS_images' and ref_value==asset_id exists in the block tree. Verify the embed renders by calling GET /workspaces/{id}/assets/{asset_id} and asserting 200.

Scenario IC-02 — Drag CKC video into note: Same as IC-01 but with content_type='video/mp4'. Assert hsLink node kind='video' in the saved doc.

Scenario IC-03 — Drag CKC moodboard card into canvas: Simulate dragging a CKC moodboard-type Loom block (a block with content_type='ckc_moodboard') onto the Loom canvas board. Expected result: POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/canvas-boards/{board_id}/placements succeeds with the moodboard block_id; GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/canvas-boards/{board_id} returns the placement with block_id == moodboard_block_id. This proves the canvas board accepts CKC-typed blocks as first-class citizens.

Scenario IC-04 — CKC character referenced in note: Create a Loom block with content_type='ckc_character'. In the note, insert a [[loom_block:character_block_id]] wikilink. Save the note. Assert: the note's block tree contains the hsLink node referencing the character block. Call GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/blocks/{character_block_id}/backlinks and assert the note's block ID appears as a backlink. This proves that wikilinks to CKC entities create real Loom backlinks.

Scenario IC-05 — Route note selection to Stage pane: Select a text range in an open note. Trigger the 'route-to-stage' command (implemented in MT-034). Assert: a Stage pane event is emitted via the command bus with the selected text and source block_id. The Stage pane (CKC surface) receives the event and displays the selection. Verify via AccessKit: the Stage pane widget with author_id='stage.selection.preview' has updated label text matching the selected text.

INTERCONNECTION EDGE 2 — Note <-> code cross-reference (test_interconnect_note_code_crossref.rs)

Scenario IC-06 — Open code block from note: In a rich-text note, insert a [[symbol:rust::src/lib.rs::my_function]] hsLink node. Click the link (simulate via AccessKit action Activate on the widget with author_id matching the hsLink node). Assert: the Code editor pane opens to the file 'src/lib.rs' and scrolls to the line range of 'my_function' as returned by GET /workspaces/{id}/code-nav/symbols/{symbol_entity_id}. Requires: a real code-nav symbol exists in PG (create one via the code-nav indexer or direct insert).

Scenario IC-07 — Reference code symbol from note: In the Code editor, place the cursor on a function name. Trigger 'Copy as note reference' (MT-033 command). Assert: the clipboard receives a string '[[symbol:rust::path/to/file.rs::function_name]]'. Paste into the note. Assert: the note's block tree gains an hsLink node of kind='symbol' with the correct ref_value. Save and reload. Assert the node persists.

Scenario IC-08 — Shared find/replace across code and note: Open the global find/replace surface (MT-029 find-in-files + rich doc find, bridged via the shared command bus from MT-031). Enter a query string that exists in BOTH a code file and a note. Assert: results contain entries from BOTH surfaces (at least one code file hit and one note hit). This proves the shared command bus routes the find request to both editor backends.

Scenario IC-09 — Code diagnostics surface note reference: Inject a fake LSP diagnostic with a 'related information' entry pointing to a note's KRD block ID. Assert: the Code editor's gutter diagnostic for that line includes a hyperlink chip referencing the note. Clicking the chip (AccessKit action on the diagnostic chip widget) triggers navigation to the note in the rich editor pane. Verify via AccessKit: after the click, the rich editor pane with author_id='rich_editor.pane.{doc_id}' gains focus.

INTERCONNECTION EDGE 3 — Loom backlink + search across surfaces (test_interconnect_loom_backlink_search.rs)

Scenario IC-10 — Backlink appears across surfaces: Create a rich-text note A (KRD-based, Loom block loom_A). In the note, add a wikilink to a code-block document B (another Loom block loom_B). Save note A. Assert: GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/blocks/{loom_B}/backlinks returns an entry whose source_block_id == loom_A. This proves that saving a note with a wikilink registers a real Loom backlink edge in PG, not a local-only reference.

Scenario IC-11 — Loom Search v2 surfaces results from both editors: Create one rich-text note and one code-file Loom block, both containing the string 'XSEARCH_PROBE'. Call POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/search-v2 with query='XSEARCH_PROBE'. Assert: hits contain BOTH block IDs. Assert: content_type_facets includes both 'knowledge_rich_document' and 'code_file' (or equivalent content types).

Scenario IC-12 — Graph view shows cross-surface edges: After IC-10, call GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/graph with depth=2 and block_id=loom_A. Assert: the graph response contains both loom_A and loom_B as nodes, with an edge connecting them. Render the graph layout in the native knowledge surface (force-directed layout call). Assert no layout panic and that both nodes appear at distinct positions.

Scenario IC-13 — AI Loom job proposes cross-surface link: Create a rich-text note whose content describes 'my_function' from a code file. Run the link-suggest AI job (POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/ai-jobs with kind='link_suggest', block_ids=[note_block_id]). Assert: at least one suggestion targets the code file's Loom block. Accept the suggestion (POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/ai-suggestions/{suggestion_id}/accept). Assert: GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/blocks/{note_block_id}/backlinks now includes the code file block. (This scenario requires a real AI model endpoint; if SKIP_AI_TESTS=1, mark #[ignore] with explicit console output.)

Scenario IC-14 — Quick-switcher surfaces items from both editors: Call GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher?q=XSEARCH_PROBE. Assert: results contain entries from BOTH the note (content_type='knowledge_rich_document') and the code block (content_type='code_file'). Assert: each result has a block_id, title, content_type, and updated_at.

INTERCONNECTION EDGE 4 — Shared undo + event-ledger across surfaces (test_interconnect_shared_undo_ledger.rs)

Scenario IC-15 — Undo crosses rich editor edit: Edit a rich-text note (insert 'EDIT_A'). Trigger global undo (Ctrl+Z via the shared undo stack implemented in MT-035). Assert: the note's content no longer contains 'EDIT_A'. Assert: GET /workspaces/{id}/knowledge/documents/{doc_id} returns the pre-edit version (optimistic concurrency version decremented or document body reverted). This proves the shared undo stack is wired to the backend save pathway.

Scenario IC-16 — Undo crosses code editor edit: Edit a code buffer (insert 'CODE_EDIT' at line 1). Trigger global undo. Assert: the buffer no longer contains 'CODE_EDIT' at line 1. Assert: if the code buffer is backed by the PG code-file store, the persisted version is also reverted.

Scenario IC-17 — Event-ledger records cross-surface actions: Perform a sequence: (1) insert text in note, (2) save note, (3) insert wikilink to code block, (4) save note again. Call GET /workspaces/{id}/events?source_block_id={note_loom_block_id}&limit=10. Assert: at least 2 KNOWLEDGE_RICH_DOCUMENT_SAVED events appear in the ledger, in order. Assert: the second event's payload contains the wikilink block reference. This proves the event-ledger captures real-time cross-surface authoring history.

Scenario IC-18 — Shared undo stack policy: scoped per-pane vs. global: Open two panes simultaneously (rich editor note A and code editor file B). Edit both. Trigger undo once. Assert: the undo reverts the MOST RECENTLY EDITED surface (not both). This proves the shared undo stack respects the per-pane scope policy declared in MT-035. The assertion checks: if the code edit was last, the code buffer reverts; the note is unchanged.

SHARED UNDO BACKEND CONTRACT: The native undo stack (MT-035) must write an undo snapshot before every save. Snapshot format: { surface: 'rich_editor' | 'code_editor', doc_id, version_before, content_before: bytes }. On undo, the system re-saves content_before to the backend. These tests verify this contract without reimplementing it (the contract is implemented in MT-035; these tests call it).

INTERCONNECT MANIFEST (src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_interconnect_manifest.json): one entry per scenario: { scenario_id, edge, description, surfaces_involved: [], backend_apis_called: [], proof_fn, status }. Updated by the test runner. 18 entries total (IC-01..IC-18).

FILES TO CREATE:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_interconnect_manifest.json
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_interconnect_ckc_to_note.rs (IC-01..IC-05)
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_interconnect_note_code_crossref.rs (IC-06..IC-09)
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_interconnect_loom_backlink_search.rs (IC-10..IC-14)
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_interconnect_shared_undo_ledger.rs (IC-15..IC-18)

STEP-BY-STEP IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Reuse the workspace fixture builder and PG setup from MT-044/MT-045.
2. For AccessKit-based interaction proofs (IC-05, IC-09): use egui_kittest to build the widget tree and simulate actions by author_id. Assert the resulting egui context has the expected focus or label state. Reuse the AccessKit registration/snapshot helpers in handshake_native::accessibility::* (mod.rs, live.rs, registry.rs, snapshot.rs).
3. For the command-bus proofs (IC-08): instantiate the shared command bus built on handshake_native::command_registry (commands) and handshake_native::event_bus (cross-surface dispatch) in-process, subscribe both editor surfaces, dispatch a FindQuery command, and collect results from both.
4. For IC-13 (AI job), gate with SKIP_AI_TESTS=1 env var and mark #[ignore] with explicit console output. Never assert on specific AI content; only assert that the suggestion list is non-empty and the accept endpoint writes a real edge.
5. IC-15 and IC-16 (undo): the test must call the real shared undo stack push(snapshot) before the edit, then undo() after, and then verify backend state via GET, not by inspecting in-memory state only.
6. All scenarios must clean up their PG rows in a DropGuard.
7. The interconnection proof files are flat integration tests under src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_interconnect_*.rs; each is automatically picked up by cargo's integration-test harness. No [[test]] stanza is required for flat tests/test_*.rs files.
8. Each test function that calls an AccessKit surface must call the accessibility flush helper in handshake_native::accessibility (e.g. accessibility::flush_pending_updates() or the snapshot refresh in accessibility::snapshot) after simulating an action, to ensure the AccessKit tree is up-to-date before asserting labels or focus.
acceptance_criteria: src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/ contains test_interconnect_manifest.json and the four scenario files (test_interconnect_ckc_to_note.rs, test_interconnect_note_code_crossref.rs, test_interconnect_loom_backlink_search.rs, test_interconnect_shared_undo_ledger.rs).
test_interconnect_manifest.json contains exactly 18 entries (IC-01..IC-18) with fields: scenario_id, edge, description, surfaces_involved, backend_apis_called, proof_fn, status.
`cargo test -p handshake-native -- interconnect` compiles and reports 18 test functions recognized by the harness.
IC-01 (CKC image into note): passes — saved doc contains hsLink node kind='HS_images' with correct asset_id; GET /assets/{asset_id} returns 200.
IC-04 (CKC character wikilink -> backlink): passes — GET /loom/blocks/{character_block_id}/backlinks contains the note's block ID.
IC-05 (route note selection to Stage): passes — AccessKit widget 'stage.selection.preview' label matches the selected text after command dispatch.
IC-06 (open code block from note): passes — Code editor pane opens to the correct file and line range; AccessKit focus assertion passes.
IC-08 (shared find/replace): passes — results contain at least one code file hit AND at least one note hit for the same query string.
IC-10 (backlink registered on save): passes — GET /loom/blocks/{loom_B}/backlinks contains loom_A after note A is saved.
IC-11 (Loom Search v2 across surfaces): passes — hits contain both block IDs; facets contain both content types.
IC-13 (AI link suggestion): either passes with a real AI endpoint or is marked #[ignore] with SKIP_AI_TESTS=1. NEVER silently skipped.
IC-15 (undo reverts rich editor edit): passes — after Ctrl+Z, GET /knowledge/documents/{doc_id} body does NOT contain 'EDIT_A'.
IC-17 (event-ledger records cross-surface actions): passes — GET /events returns >= 2 KNOWLEDGE_RICH_DOCUMENT_SAVED events with correct payloads.
IC-18 (undo scope policy): passes — undo reverts only the most recently edited surface; the other surface is unchanged.
After a full passing run, test_interconnect_manifest.json has 0 FAIL entries.
All tests are idempotent — a second run produces the same results.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native -- interconnect --nocapture 2>&1 | tee /tmp/interconnect_run.txt && grep -c 'test interconnect_' /tmp/interconnect_run.txt | grep -q '^18'

cat src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_interconnect_manifest.json | python -c "import sys,json; m=json.load(sys.stdin); fails=[e for e in m if e['status']=='FAIL']; print('FAIL:',len(fails)); assert len(fails)==0"
implementation_notes: CROSS-WP DEPENDENCY (WP-011): The interconnection proofs reuse foundation surfaces delivered by WP-KERNEL-011 — the command registry/event bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs), command palette (command_palette.rs + command_registry.rs), context menus (context_menu.rs + context_menu_surfaces.rs), the Loom canvas board (canvas_board.rs) and graph (loom_graph.rs), settings (settings_dialog.rs + workspace_settings.rs), AccessKit (accessibility/*), and MCP tools (mcp/*). These WP-011 modules already exist under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/ and are reused, not recreated. This cross-WP dependency on WP-KERNEL-011 is recorded here as prose; lifecycle.depends_on lists only intra-WP MT ids.

EDGE 1 (CKC->note): The native InsertEmbed editor command lives in the WP-012 rich_editor module created under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/ (EditorCommand::InsertEmbed). Call it directly in the test, passing the HsLinkKind and ref_value. Then call the rich editor save() which dispatches to PUT /workspaces/{id}/knowledge/documents/{doc_id}. Use the axum test client for the save, not a real HTTP connection.

EDGE 1 IC-03 (moodboard on canvas): Create a Loom block with content_type='ckc_moodboard' via POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/blocks. Then call POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/canvas-boards/{board_id}/placements with { block_id: moodboard_block_id, x: 100.0, y: 100.0 }. The canvas surface reuses the existing handshake_native::canvas_board module. The acceptance test is that the placement is returned in GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/canvas-boards/{board_id}.

EDGE 2 (note<->code): The cross-surface navigation (IC-06, IC-09) reuses the shared command bus built on handshake_native::command_registry (commands) and handshake_native::event_bus (cross-surface dispatch), instantiated with both editor surfaces registered. In the test, create the bus, attach a MockCodeEditorReceiver and MockRichEditorReceiver that record received commands. Send the NavigateTo command. Assert the correct receiver recorded the navigation. Then verify with the AccessKit helpers in handshake_native::accessibility that focus moved to the correct pane.

EDGE 2 IC-07 (copy as note reference): The 'Copy as note reference' command is implemented in the WP-012 code_editor module created under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/ (CodeEditorCommand::CopyAsNoteReference). It must write to the system clipboard. In the test, use the egui_kittest clipboard capture (or a test-only ClipboardCapture trait) to intercept the clipboard write without touching the real OS clipboard.

EDGE 3 IC-11 (search across surfaces): The two Loom blocks (note and code) must both be indexed by the search backend before the query. The native search surface reuses handshake_native::search_rail. Verify indexing by calling POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/search-v2 immediately after creation and checking that both block IDs appear. If indexing is async, poll with a 5-attempt retry with 200ms sleep (budget: 1 s total) before asserting.
EDGE 3 IC-12 (graph): The native graph render reuses handshake_native::loom_graph for the force-directed layout call. Assert no layout panic and that both nodes appear at distinct positions.

EDGE 4 IC-15/IC-16 (undo): The shared undo stack (MT-035) must be the SAME instance shared between the code editor and the rich editor. In the test, create one undo stack, pass it (Arc<Mutex<...>>) to both editor instances. After both edits, pop undo once and assert only the last edit is reverted.

EDGE 4 IC-17 (event-ledger): The event-ledger endpoint is GET /workspaces/{id}/events (or the Flight Recorder query endpoint, whichever exists). PostgreSQL EventLedger only; there is NO SQLite path. The backend is reused via API only; do not modify src/backend/**. If the endpoint accepts a source_block_id query param, use it. If not, query all events and filter by block_id client-side.

For IC-05 (AccessKit Stage pane check): the Stage pane must have a stable author_id registered in MT-034 (the WP-012 CKC stage widget under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/, with author_id='stage.selection.preview', registered via handshake_native::accessibility::registry). If MT-034 did not register this id, this test surfaces a gap and must be marked with a typed blocker comment rather than silently asserting on a stale label.
All scenario functions follow the naming pattern: interconnect_ic{NN}_{snake_case_description}. The manifest proof_fn field must match exactly.

SKIP_AI_TESTS=1 handling for IC-13: use #[cfg_attr(not(feature = 'ai_tests'), ignore)] or check the env var at test start with std::env::var('SKIP_AI_TESTS').is_ok() and call return; with a println! before returning.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/HsLinkView.tsx — CKC embed NodeView pattern (IC-01, IC-02, IC-04): the [[HS_images:assetId]], [[video:assetId]], [[loom_block:blockId]] node kinds and their resolution states (resolving/resolved/failed) that the native editor must replicate

app/src/components/LoomTransclusionView.tsx — transclusion read-through resolution pattern; the cycle-detection gap (the React version has no cycle detection) is a native improvement this MT must prove
app/src/components/LoomSearchV2Panel.tsx — cross-surface search UI pattern (IC-11, IC-14): content_type facets, hit rendering, save-results-as-view flow
app/src/components/LoomAiReviewPanel.tsx — AI Loom job + suggestion accept/reject pattern (IC-13): runLoomAiJob, listLoomAiSuggestions, acceptLoomAiSuggestion, acceptAllLoomAiSuggestions
app/src/components/CodeSymbolPanel.tsx — code symbol nav surface (IC-06, IC-07): getCodeSymbol, getCodeFileLens, symbolFilePath extraction pattern

app/src/components/BlockCollectionView.tsx — saved-view query pattern (IC-11 saved-view follow-on): queryBlockViewResults, updateBlockView — proves search-results-as-saved-view can be queried cross-surface
app/src/lib/editor/link_navigation.ts — dispatchHsLinkNavigate (IC-06): the event dispatch pattern for inter-surface navigation that the native command bus must replicate
binds_backend_api: PUT /workspaces/{workspace_id}/knowledge/documents/{doc_id} — save note with embed/wikilink (IC-01, IC-02, IC-04, IC-06, IC-07, IC-15)
GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/assets/{asset_id} — verify embedded asset bytes (IC-01, IC-02)
POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks — create Loom blocks (IC-03 moodboard, IC-04 character, IC-10, IC-11)
GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/graph — graph topology for cross-surface edges (IC-12)
GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks/{block_id}/knowledge — EventLedger authority bridge (IC-17 event-ledger check)
GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/code-nav/symbols/{symbol_entity_id} — code symbol definition for note reference (IC-06)

risks: RISK-1: Surfaces never actually share state — the 'interconnection' tests pass because each surface uses its own independent backend call that happens to return the same data, not because they share a command bus or undo stack. The tests would not catch a broken command bus.

RISK-2: Backlink not written on save — saving a note with a wikilink via the native editor does not actually call the backlink upsert endpoint, so IC-10 passes trivially (no backlink to check) or fails with an empty backlinks list.
RISK-3: AccessKit proofs are shallow — IC-05 and IC-09 check an author_id label but not that the ACTUAL SURFACE RENDERED the content, because egui_kittest does not run a GPU frame.

RISK-4: Undo stack scope policy (IC-18) is not tested with truly concurrent edits — the test edits surface A then surface B sequentially, so 'undo reverts the last edit' is trivially true even with two independent undo stacks.
RISK-5: AI test (IC-13) is silently skipped — SKIP_AI_TESTS=1 is set by default in CI and nobody notices that the cross-surface AI link edge is never proven.

RISK-6: Search indexing is async (IC-11) — the test creates blocks and immediately queries; the search index may not have processed the new blocks yet, causing a false FAIL on a fast machine that indexed before the first query attempt.

RISK-7: Event-ledger endpoint does not exist — GET /workspaces/{id}/events or the Flight Recorder query endpoint is not yet implemented in handshake_core (it may be planned but not delivered); IC-17 would need to be a typed blocker.

RISK-8: CKC content types not yet defined — the 'ckc_moodboard' and 'ckc_character' content_type values used in IC-03 and IC-04 may not be declared in the LoomBlockContentType enum yet, causing a validation error.
minimum_controls: CTRL-1 (RISK-1): Each cross-surface test must use the SAME command bus / undo stack INSTANCE for both surfaces. Use Arc<Mutex<T>> and assert the bus's internal dispatch count incremented, not just that the target surface received the event.

CTRL-2 (RISK-2): In IC-10, explicitly call the native editor's save() API (which should call PUT /knowledge/documents + backlink upsert), then wait for the response before querying backlinks. If the native save does NOT call the backlink endpoint, the test surfaces this as a typed blocker with a clear error message.

CTRL-3 (RISK-3): For AccessKit proofs, use egui_kittest to simulate a full widget tree frame (ctx.run_ui()) after the command dispatch, then assert the label. Document that this is a layout-level proof (not GPU-render-level) and note what additional screenshot proof would look like.

CTRL-4 (RISK-4): IC-18 must use a single Arc<Mutex<UndoStack>> shared by both editors. After sequential edits (A then B), call undo() once. Then assert: B is reverted AND A is NOT reverted. The test must explicitly inspect both surfaces after one undo call.

CTRL-5 (RISK-5): IC-13 must print 'AI INTERCONNECT TEST SKIPPED: SKIP_AI_TESTS=1' to stdout when skipped, and the test_interconnect_manifest.json entry must have status='SKIPPED' not 'PASS'. A CI pipeline that has all SKIPPED AI entries must alert the operator.

CTRL-6 (RISK-6): IC-11 must poll the search endpoint up to 5 times with 200ms sleep before asserting, to tolerate async indexing. Document the polling behavior and the maximum wait (1 s). If after 5 attempts the blocks are still not indexed, fail with 'search_index_not_ready' error, not a trivially passing assertion.

CTRL-7 (RISK-7): Before authoring IC-17, verify the event-ledger query endpoint exists in src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/mod.rs. If it does not exist, IC-17 must be a typed blocker entry in the manifest (status='BLOCKED', blocker='event_ledger_query_endpoint_missing') rather than a silently passing test.

CTRL-8 (RISK-8): Before authoring IC-03 and IC-04, verify that 'ckc_moodboard' and 'ckc_character' are valid values in the LoomBlockContentType enum (check src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/mod.rs or the LoomBlockContentType definition). If they are not yet defined, surface as a typed blocker and use a generic 'note' content_type as a fallback, marking the scenario as PARTIAL.
